sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation

print("Before SPACE nodes:")
for node in prev_seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, hash={node['hash']}")

print("\nAfter SPACE nodes:")
for node in curr_seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={node['id']}, color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, hash={node['hash']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The "SSSSS" is at cols 19-23 (5 cells). But the left square is at cols 24-28, which would be "BBBBB" (black). That doesn't make sense.

Or maybe the goal is to move them to the "head" positions at the top of each figure.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try to move both squares to the top
# Current: L=(44-48, 24-28), R=(49-53, 34-38)
# Try UP multiple times
for i in range(5):
    result = action(['UP'])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    squares = [(n['boundary'], n['color']) for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S']
    print(f"UP {i+1}: changed={result.get('board_changed')}, squares={squares}")
    if result.get('level_completed') or result.get('done'):
        print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
